summary: Neo/specialist clouds (CoreWeave, Crusoe, Nebius, Lambda) represent a durable business model, not a transitional arbitrage
summary_extended: Baker argues that neo/specialist cloud providers — companies like CoreWeave, Crusoe, Nebius, and Lambda — represent a structurally durable business model rather than a transitional arbitrage that will eventually be displaced by hyperscalers. His core reasoning is that running GPU clusters at high utilization is operationally extremely difficult, analogous to piloting a Formula 1 car, and that CoreWeave in particular achieves 2–3x higher GPU utilization per hour than lower-quality competitors, constituting a genuine and defensible operational moat. He contends that hyperscalers were historically locked in a cost-minimization mentality and are only now beginning the cultural shift required to compete effectively in this space, meaning the window for specialist operators remains open. Atreides holds a disclosed large position in Crusoe, and Baker notes he was conflicted out of CoreWeave at a $1.1B valuation, underscoring his long-term conviction in the category.
detail: Baker argues running GPU clusters at high utilization is extremely difficult — analogous to driving a Formula 1 car. CoreWeave GPUs are utilized 2–3x more per hour than lower-quality providers. This operational excellence is a durable competitive advantage. Hyperscalers were historically in a cost mentality and are only now making the cultural shift. Atreides has a large position in Crusoe. Baker was conflicted out of CoreWeave at $1.1B valuation.
confidence_evidence: Baker has a disclosed large position in Crusoe, frames the category as structurally durable with a specific operational moat argument, and uses extended analogy to make the case
quote_fragment: it is durable and the hyper scalers for a long time were stuck in a cost mentality

ticker_audit.reasoning: CoreWeave is explicitly named as the primary example of operational excellence in GPU utilization, with specific 2-3x utilization data cited. It is a direct subject of the thesis.

Crusoe Energy (CRUS) is a semiconductor company unrelated to Crusoe the cloud provider. The implied ticker for Crusoe cloud is not US-listed as a public equity under a clean ticker — removing to avoid misidentification.

Nebius (NBIS) is explicitly named as one of the neo/specialist cloud providers that constitute the durable category Baker is describing. It is a direct subject of the thesis and is US-listed on Nasdaq.
Lambda Labs is not publicly listed; there is no US-listed equity ticker for Lambda Labs. Removing to avoid misidentification with unrelated tickers.
